_about: Template gold set for the IR retrieval-eval harness (src/analytics/ir_eval.py).
Copy this to your own file, replace the example queries, and load it with
ir_eval.load_gold_set(path). It is corpus-specific + human-judged, so it is NOT
bundled with real data -- only you can grade YOUR articles.
How to grade: for each query, list the article ids you have judged and a GRADED
relevance 0/1/2 -- 0 = irrelevant, 1 = relevant, 2 = highly relevant. The doc id is
the Article.id (a number); JSON object keys are strings, which is fine (the loader
stringifies ids on both sides). 'language' and 'axis' are optional (default en/topic)
and let the report break results down per language + per retrieval phenomenon
(known-item / topic / cross-lingual / near-dup) instead of one pooled average.
Then: evaluate_against_corpus(session, gold) scores the live search; or
bm25f_weight_ab(session, gold, weights_a=(1,1), weights_b=(4,1)) A/Bs the BM25F
title/body weights over YOUR judgements so the P5.1 default is chosen on evidence,
not guessed. The keys below (the _about block and any unknown keys) are ignored.
Pool the judged docs and keep a single assessor consistent (Voorhees).
